message.void_echo.warden_ending: The void falls silent. The Echo Warden is defeated — the ancient darkness has been lifted. You succeeded where the builders of this world failed. But the void whispers one final question: at what cost?

message.void_echo.warden_already_exists: The Echo Warden already lurks nearby!
message.void_echo.echo_shard_1: A voice echoes: 'I am Aerion IV, last king of Aerolith. This sanctum is our final seal against the Void. If you hear this — we have already failed.'
message.void_echo.echo_shard_2: The memory whispers: 'Kaelen — my dearest friend, my royal guard — took the Void into himself to save me. He is still in there. The creature you hunt was once the noblest of us.'
message.void_echo.echo_shard_3: An ancient record: 'The Void feeds on memory. Our crystals can hold memory, but they cannot hold forever. The fortress endured for centuries — but centuries are not eternity.'
message.void_echo.echo_shard_4: Fragments of will: 'I sealed myself inside the crystal to hold the Void gate shut. Every passing year, I lose more of who I was. Summon me only if you are ready to end this — one way or another.'
message.void_echo.echo_shard_5: The final truth: 'We — Aerolith — failed. But failure is not the end. You carry the echo of everything we were. The Void whispers your name now, traveler. Do not let it feed on you as it fed on us.'
book.void_echo.last_architect.title: Journal of the Last Architect
book.void_echo.last_architect.author: Architect Seren
book.void_echo.last_architect.page1:
I am Seren, last of the Royal Shapers. By the order of King Aerion IV, I designed this Altar to serve as the bridge between worlds — a passage from the overworld into the Void's End.

Our king believed that one day, someone would need to cross over. Not an Aerolithian — we had sealed our fate. But someone FROM BEYOND. Someone who did not grow up hearing the Void's whispers.

That someone is you.
book.void_echo.last_architect.page2:
The Void Key you hold was forged from the last crystal I ever shaped. It resonates with the portal frame because it carries my memory — my knowledge of the bridge between worlds.

Do not lose it. I have no more crystals left to shape another.

The Altar will recognize the key. Step through. The Void's End awaits.
book.void_echo.last_architect.page3:
One final warning: The Void feeds on memories. Every moment you spend in the Void's End, it learns a little more about you. The creatures there — the worms, the wraiths, the guard — they are drawn to memory like moths to flame.

Travel light. Stay alert. And do not linger where the whispers grow loud.

May the crystals guide your path.

— Seren
book.void_echo.commanders_entry.title: Commander's Final Entry
book.void_echo.commanders_entry.author: Commander Kaelen
book.void_echo.commanders_entry.page1:
I can feel it now. The Void is inside me.

We repelled the seventh tide — seven waves of darkness crashing against the fortress walls, and we held. My soldiers cheered. Aerion embraced me. But during the battle, something got through. I absorbed it deliberately — the king would have died otherwise.

I thought I could contain it. I was wrong.
book.void_echo.commanders_entry.page2:
The whispers started three days ago. At first, they sounded like battle commands — strategies, formations, enemy movements. I thought it was instinct. Then I realized the whispers were describing how to kill my own soldiers.

The Void doesn't give you orders. It shows you possibilities. And the worst possibilities are the ones that make tactical sense.

I am stepping down from command today. Aerion doesn't know why. I told him I was tired.
book.void_echo.commanders_entry.page3:
If you are reading this, I am already gone. The creature you will face in the depths of this fortress — the Void Stalker — that is what remains of me.

Strike true. Do not hesitate. The part of me that is still Kaelen wants to die as a soldier, not as a monster.

Aerion — if somehow you read this — forgive me. You were the best king our people ever had. I hope your seal holds longer than my willpower did.

— Kaelen
book.void_echo.summoning_ritual.title: The Summoning Ritual
book.void_echo.summoning_ritual.author: High Shaper Miriel
book.void_echo.summoning_ritual.page1:
To whomever finds this sanctum: what you stand before is not a tomb. It is a prison — and a choice.

King Aerion IV sealed his consciousness inside the great crystal at the center of this chamber. Four smaller crystals amplify the seal, one at each cardinal direction. As long as they stand, the Void's main entrance cannot fully open.
book.void_echo.summoning_ritual.page2:
The ritual requires the heart of the Void Stalker — Kaelen, our fallen commander. His corrupted crystal heart resonates with the king's seal. When placed upon the Echo Altar with all four amplifier crystals intact, the seal will break — and Aerion will awaken.

You must understand what this means: the Warden is not your enemy. He has been holding the Void at bay for centuries, alone, with nothing but his will.
book.void_echo.summoning_ritual.page3:
Once awakened, Aerion cannot be put back to sleep. The seal will be broken permanently. If you defeat him, the Void will flow freely — no longer contained. If he defeats you, the Void gains a champion.

There is no victory here. Only a choice between endings.

Summon him only if you are prepared to live with whichever ending you create.

— Miriel, High Shaper of Aerolith
